sections.paragraph:
ἀρνέομαι [etym. unclear]

—1. ‘give a negative answer to’, say no, deny Mt 26:70; Lk 8:45; J 1:20; 1 J 2:22.

—2. ‘refuse to acknowledge’

—a. of pers. disown, deny Mt 10:33; Ac 3:14; 7:35; Tit 1:16; Jd 4; cp. 1 J 2:22; in wordplay 2 Ti 2:13 (cp. vs. 12 and contrast the phrase ἀ. ἕαυτον in 3 below).

—b. of things deny, disown 1 Ti 5:8; 2 Ti 3:5; Tit 2:12.

—3. ‘refuse to pay any attention to’, disregard, ignore, disdain ἀ. ἕαυτον deny/disregard oneself = refuse to indulge one’s own interests Lk 9:23; ἀ. λέγεσϑαι υἱός refused/disdained to be known as son of Pharaoh’s daughter Hb 11:24.
